example_prompt: Retrieve a single Flutterwave customer by their unique customer ID.

id.description: Unique customer identifier (path parameter, e.g. cus_3XarBILKQS) returned by create_customer or list_customers.

name.description: Present only when provided as nested object during customer creation.

phone.description: Present only when provided as nested object during customer creation.

address.description: Present only when provided during customer creation.

gotchas: Flutterwave v4 uses OAuth2 client credentials. POST client_id and client_secret as form-encoded body to https://idp.flutterwave.com/realms/flutterwave/protocol/openid-connect/token to obtain an access_token (valid 10 minutes), then use it as Bearer in the Authorization header. The FLUTTERWAVE_CLIENT_CREDENTIALS env var stores base64(client_id:client_secret).
Customer IDs returned by Flutterwave are opaque strings (e.g. cus_3XarBILKQS). Do not apply encodeURIComponent to these IDs when constructing the path — pass them in the URL as-is.
Always send an X-Trace-Id header (12-255 chars) so Flutterwave support can find the request in their logs if anything goes wrong.
An unknown customer ID returns HTTP 404 with error.type 'NOT_FOUND' and code '10404', not an empty 200. Handle 404 separately from network errors.
Validation error items use the key 'field_name' (not 'field') — match on error.validation_errors[].field_name when surfacing per-field errors to users.
